Indicative Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Bright Quarry Energy Ltd by Harbour Finch Pty Ltd (Australia).
Structure: off-market takeover bid under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth), subject to compliance with ASIC requirements and ASX Listing Rules where applicable.

Costs and break fee: each party bears its own costs; any break fee to be modest and within prevailing takeovers guidance; internal drafts may display invalid reference tags (e.g., REF-0000-XX) with no legal effect.

must_detect.matched_text: Indicative Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Bright Quarry Energy Ltd by Harbour Finch Pty Ltd (Australia).
Structure: off-market takeover bid under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth), subject to compliance with ASIC requirements and ASX Listing Rules where applicable.

Costs and break fee: each party bears its own costs; any break fee to be modest and within prevailing takeovers guidance; internal drafts may display invalid reference tags (e.g., REF-0000-XX) with no legal effect.

Indicative Term Sheet — Proposed acquisition of Bright Quarry Energy Ltd by Harbour Finch Pty Ltd (Australia).

Structure: off-market takeover bid under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth), subject to compliance with ASIC requirements and ASX Listing Rules where applicable.

Consideration: mix of cash and scrip in Harbour Finch Holdings Ltd (unlisted); no specific amounts or ratios are set in this indicative summary.

Conditions precedent: minimum 50.1% acceptance, no material adverse change, required court/ASIC processes for compulsory acquisition if relevant, and customary competition clearance.

Due diligence: confirmatory review of public and non-confidential materials via a clean-room portal; data-room access protocols are designed to avoid transfer of MNPI except to a designated clean team.

Funding: committed facilities from Inland Gulf Bank Ltd (fictional) subject to standard drawdown conditions, with no recourse to consumer credit files or health records.

Employment: no immediate changes to awards or enterprise agreements; select retention arrangements may be offered; HR templates may display placeholders such as “Date of Birth: DD/MM/YYYY” and “Role: [insert title]” only.

Privacy: compliance with the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) is required; this term sheet does not request Medicare, TFN, driver licence, or other government identifier details.

For system integration testing only, a single dummy staff record was used: a warehouse technician based in North Waradoo, WA, birth month 03/1988, device tag NW-TAB-07, payroll BSB 000-000 with masked account …4321, employed within Bright Quarry Energy operations.

ASX announcements: only already released, non-price-sensitive summaries of the proposal may be referenced; a mock illustrative release dated 01/01/2020 is acceptable for drafting.

No MNPI: the parties acknowledge section 1043A insider trading prohibitions and confirm any “forecast” or “pipeline” references in drafts are illustrative and not intended to be relied upon by the market.

Timetable: bidder’s statement to be lodged with ASIC and dispatched within statutory timeframes; target’s statement to follow in accordance with the Corporations Act (Cth); any acceleration is subject to formal relief.

Warranties: customary business warranties and indemnities; no representations will be sought or given regarding employee health information, credit scores, or family status.

Costs and break fee: each party bears its own costs; any break fee to be modest and within prevailing takeovers guidance; internal drafts may display invalid reference tags (e.g., REF-0000-XX) with no legal effect.

uncertain.reason: Device tag may function as a device serial number, but it is not explicitly labeled as a serial number.
BSB is a bank routing code; inclusion under bank_account may be debatable.
Masked digits may still constitute a bank account identifier; partial/obfuscated nature makes classification uncertain.
Indicates use of a clean room; mapping to information_barrier_marker may depend on detector vocabulary.
Signals disclosure constraints; could be treated as selective disclosure risk or general compliance language.
Heading declares absence of MNPI; detectors may not flag headings or may require the 'not generally available' wording.
